message.content: Adversaries may exploit stolen application access tokens to bypass standard authentication and access restricted accounts, information, or services. These tokens, often used in cloud and SaaS environments, authorize API requests on behalf of users or services. Misconfigured permissions can allow adversaries to request tokens with elevated privileges, leading to initial access or privilege escalation. OAuth is a common framework for issuing these tokens, which, if compromised, can grant long-term access to user accounts and associated services. This method circumvents multi-factor authentication and password changes, making detection challenging. Notable groups like APT28 and APT29 have used OAuth tokens to access email accounts and modify cloud environments. Mitigation strategies include strict permission configurations, regular token audits, and monitoring for unusual API activity.
